2606.30113.title: SA-VLA: State-aware tokenizer for improving Vision-Language-Action Models' performance
2606.30113.paper_summary: Discrete action tokenization provides a compact interface for autoregressive VLA policies, but accurately recovering continuous robot actions from discrete codes remains challenging. Existing tokenizers typically map each discrete code to a fixed continuous action prototype, ignoring the robot's current proprioceptive state. This limitation is particularly pronounced in manipulation, where the same action token may require different continuous controls under different joint configurations, object poses, and contact conditions. We therefore propose SA-VLA, a state-aware action tokenizer that conditions action decoding on robot state. We study two state-injection mechanisms for VQ-based action tokenization: cross-attention between state and action features, and a lightweight state adapter that predicts action-wise modulation factors for state-conditioned action modulation and reconstruction. The adapter formulation expands the effective support of a finite codebook by allowing each discrete token to represent a family of state-dependent continuous actions, while preserving the efficiency and compatibility of discrete action modeling. Integrated into an LLM-based VLA policy, SA-VLA supports both autoregressive and parallel action-token decoding with minimal changes to the model interface. On 12 RoboTwin manipulation tasks, SA-VLA improves the average success rate from 0.29 to 0.56 over the strongest tokenizer baseline. In zero-shot sim-to-real experiments on three real-world tasks, it further improves average success from 0.15 to 0.33 over the strongest tokenizer baseline. These results demonstrate that state-conditioned action decoding is a simple and effective mechanism for reducing the compression gap in discrete VLA policies.
2606.30113.paper_summary_zh: 離散行動標記化為自回歸 VLA 策略提供了一個緊湊的介面，但從離散代碼準確恢復連續機器人行動仍然具有挑戰性。現有的標記器通常將每個離散代碼映射到一個固定的連續行動原型，忽略了機器人當前的本體感知狀態。這一限制在操作中尤為明顯，因為相同的行動標記在不同的關節配置、物體姿勢和接觸條件下可能需要不同的連續控制。因此，我們提出了 SA-VLA，一種狀態感知的行動標記器，該標記器在行動解碼時考慮機器人狀態。我們研究了兩種基於 VQ 的行動標記化的狀態注入機制：狀態和行動特徵之間的交叉注意力，以及一種輕量級狀態適配器，該適配器預測針對狀態條件的行動調製和重建的行動級調製因子。適配器的公式擴展了有限代碼本的有效支持，讓每個離散標記能夠表示一系列依賴於狀態的連續行動，同時保持離散行動建模的效率和兼容性。整合到基於 LLM 的 VLA 策略中，SA-VLA 支持自回歸和並行行動標記解碼，對模型介面的改動最小。在 12 個 RoboTwin 操作任務中，SA-VLA 將平均成功率從 0.29 提高到 0.56，超過最強標記器的基準。在三個真實世界任務的零樣本模擬到現實實驗中，它進一步將平均成功率從 0.15 提高到 0.33，超過最強標記器的基準。這些結果表明，狀態條件的行動解碼是一種簡單而有效的機制，用於減少離散 VLA 策略中的壓縮差距。
2606.30113.author: Tengyue Jiang et.al.
2606.30113.authors: Tengyue Jiang, Chunpu Xu, Jiayue Kang, Yao Mu
